summary: Iteration 3 testing complete. Rate-limit on POST /api/inquiries, GET /api/inquiries/analytics endpoint, admin analytics panel (empty + populated states), and footer email update all verified. Backend: 10/10 new pytest tests pass (rate-limit 5-then-429, different IP not blocked, no-XFF fallback path, analytics 401 w/o auth, analytics shape & 7-day daily structure, top_products aggregation ordering & null exclusion, _rate_ip leak check) + 35/35 regression suite pass (auth, products, blogs, inquiries CRUD). Frontend: footer mailto:kartk.vani@gmail.com present on home; admin Inquiries tab shows empty-state card with NO analytics panel when total=0; after seeding 3 inquiries, panel renders 3 cards (dark Total KPI, Last 7 Days bar chart with today column = 3, Most Inquired list w/ proportional bars, top-5 truncation). Cleanup complete — DB has 0 inquiries after run.

issues: Pre-existing from iteration 2: a few Unsplash URLs for 'Potted English Lavender', 'Rosemary for Memory', 'Sleep Well Roll-On' return 404/broken image. Cosmetic only.

action_items: (Optional, low priority) Add data-testid attributes on analytics sub-elements (daily-bar, top-product-row, analytics-total KPI) to make per-element Playwright assertions easier; current parent-level testid admin-inquiry-analytics is sufficient but sub-element hooks would strengthen future automation.
(Pre-existing from iter 2) Replace broken product image URLs.
critical_code_review_comments: Rate-limit implementation is simple & correct — uses X-Forwarded-For first entry then falls back to request.client.host. Lookback window (10 min, >=5 docs) matches spec.

_rate_ip is a private DB-only field — correctly excluded from POST response (doc.pop), GET list response ({_rate_ip:0} projection), PATCH response ({_id:0} — but not explicit _rate_ip:0 on find_one at server.py:473 — LOW RISK since response_model=Inquiry will strip unknown fields, verified via test).
Analytics aggregation correctly $match filters out null product_name before $group; daily array always returns 7 entries covering the trailing week including today regardless of data sparsity.
Rate-limit is per-IP only — a bot can trivially rotate IPs. Consider coupling with a honeypot field or simple captcha if abuse appears. Not blocking.
AdminDashboard.jsx now ~450 lines with InquiryAnalytics inline. Still under 700 cap, but if more admin features land, extract InquiryAnalytics to its own file.
No index on {_rate_ip, created_at} compound — current index on _rate_ip alone is fine at current scale; if inquiry volume grows, a compound index would speed the rate-limit count query.
seed_data_creation: Temporarily seeded ~11 test inquiries during rate-limit + aggregation + UI tests. All cleaned up via DELETE /api/inquiries/{id}. Final DB state: 0 inquiries. Resend sent live emails to kartk.vani@gmail.com (expected — well under 30 total as requested).
context_for_next_testing_agent: New test file /app/backend/tests/test_inquiries_analytics.py covers rate-limit + analytics comprehensively. Run all three test files with REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL set (pytest must have the env var): `cd /app/backend && REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> python -m pytest tests/ -v`. The rate-limit uses a 10-min rolling window on {_rate_ip, created_at}; tests use TEST-NET reserved IPs (203.0.113.x, 198.51.100.x) via X-Forwarded-For to get distinct buckets per test. Analytics returns top_products sorted desc by count with null product_name excluded, and daily is always exactly 7 entries ending on today. Footer email link is in /app/frontend/src/components/Footer.jsx.
